behaviors.description: Exclude out-of-stock products regardless of relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is filter->ranker interaction: a high-scoring item must still be removed after availability filtering. MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module handoff, not one pure function. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess stated; no AI/ML output dependency; no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text only says 'out-of-stock are excluded' and does not require real HTTP, subprocess, or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire catalog source, inventory status, and ranking pipeline in-process; seed one out-of-stock item with highest score; assert it never appears in returned recommendations.

behaviors.description: Exclude categories explicitly blocked in user settings.
behaviors.justification: Failure occurs at user-settings->preference-filter->result pipeline boundary. A unit test of predicate logic can miss settings loading/mapping bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no AI output quality, no multi-step agent loop. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic; text is deterministic rule on blocked categories.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process user profile store plus recommendation pipeline; mark category blocked; include otherwise relevant products from blocked/unblocked categories; assert blocked category items are absent.

behaviors.description: Rank products with recent browsing signals above products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text targets algorithm ranking rule itself ('recommendation algorithm ranks...higher'), which can be validated at scorer/ranker function boundary with deterministic feature vectors. MOCK-MISS: no explicit module handoff required by wording. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior does not mention state propagation across modules, only ranking logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call ranking/scoring function with controlled product feature inputs where only browsing-signal differs; assert higher score/order for browsing-matched products.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns correct page with stable ordering when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is sorter->paginator->query/result assembly interaction; stability bugs often come from tie-breaking across modules. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no explicit OS/network/subprocess need, no AI dependency, no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Unit because page stability across repeated requests requires combined ordering + pagination behavior, not a single local function only.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommendation pipeline twice against unchanged fixture data; request page 2 both times; assert identical ordered IDs and correct slice boundaries.

behaviors.description: Ensure at least one recommendation from an unseen category (serendipity).
behaviors.justification: This is typically a ranker->diversification/post-processing handoff rule; failures appear when final list assembly drops exploration candidates. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real infra, no LLM/ML nondeterministic call implied, no multi-agent sequence. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Unit because behavior is about final surfaced list composition, usually beyond a single scoring function.
behaviors.test_description: With user history covering categories A/B and candidate pool including C, run full in-process ranking+diversification pipeline and assert at least one top-N result is from unseen category C.

behaviors.description: Cold-start user gets popularity-based recommendations, not empty results.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is history-detection->fallback-strategy->result generation. Unit tests of popularity sort alone miss empty-history routing bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required by text; no AI output quality requirement; no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior is deterministic fallback logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline with user profile lacking purchase/browse history; verify non-empty output and that ranking aligns with popularity baseline fixture.

behaviors.description: Click tracking stores product ID, list position, and timestamp per interaction.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at event capture->tracking payload->persistence boundary; field mapping/order bugs are cross-module. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: can be caught with in-process store + controllable clock; no required real OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior does not require real external analytics infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate click events from recommendation list in an integrated test with repository and fake clock; assert persisted record has exact product_id, rank position, and expected timestamp.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: Primary boundary is full in-process pipeline performance across modules and data structures. No explicit requirement for real HTTP/subprocess/OS primitives beyond normal runtime. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no AI output dependency and no agentic sequence. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text specifies engine latency target, not external network/process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Construct 100k-product fixture and execute end-to-end in-process recommendation path under controlled benchmark settings; assert p95 (or defined metric) under 200ms.

behaviors.description: After purchase, previously recommended product is deprioritized in later requests.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is purchase event/state update->subsequent ranking handoff across requests. Unit ranking logic alone misses stale-state propagation. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess, no LLM dependency, no multi-agent sequence. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Unit because behavior explicitly spans 'subsequent requests', requiring state flow between modules.
behaviors.test_description: Generate initial recommendations, record a purchased product, then request recommendations again with updated user state; assert purchased item rank drops below prior position (or outside threshold).

behaviors.description: Same user state yields deterministic identical ranked list across repeated requests.
behaviors.justification: Determinism here is request-pipeline level (candidate retrieval, sorting, tie-breakers, and any seeded randomness). Cross-module interactions can introduce nondeterminism. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real infra requirement stated, no AI/ML output quality dependency, no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Unit because behavior compares full request outputs across calls, not one local function.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommendation endpoint/service twice with identical user state and unchanged data snapshot; assert exact ordered product ID list equality.
